**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Nanjing Xin Xiaofei Electronic Technology Co., Ltd. presents an exemplary case of a "two-person digital repair shop in Zhujiang Road computer market" being absurdly elevated by the system to a "major power transmission and distribution equipment manufacturing enterprise," serving as a critical instance of classification dimensionality reduction. The forensic engine has completely purged the underlying data contamination stemming from false multinational consumer electronics OEM involvement with Apple Inc. The official industrial and commercial registration system was completely misled by semantic matching: merely because its name included "Electronic Technology" and its declared business scope potentially involved "electromechanical products," it was crudely classified into the capital-intensive, high-risk sector of "Electrical Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing - Power Transmission and Distribution and Control Equipment Manufacturing." However, physical probes and the common sense engine immediately executed a comprehensive deconstruction: this entity is a sole proprietorship micro-enterprise established in 2010, with a paid-in capital of only 510,000 RMB, and its 2024 social insurance participation count registers an exceptionally meager "2 individuals"! The most conclusive disproof originated from its registered coordinates: "Building A, Weilai City, No. 488 Zhujiang Road, Xuanwu District, Nanjing City." "Weilai City" is a long-established electronic marketplace and mixed-use commercial/residential complex situated on Zhujiang Road, renowned as Nanjing's prominent street for computers and digital products. To establish a heavy industrial production line for manufacturing large "power transmission and distribution control cabinets (substation equipment)" within a bustling computer market, with merely an owner and a single assistant, utilizing only 510,000 RMB? This not only defies physical common sense but constitutes a humorous deconstruction of urban power grid security. A review of its highly pragmatic authentic business license reveals it is replete with "sales of computers, printers, digital products, office supplies" and "network engineering installation and maintenance (cabling)." The diagnostic conclusion is unequivocal: this is unequivocally not a power transmission and distribution manufacturing plant, but a genuine "two-person micro-enterprise engaged in computer supplies arbitrage and low-voltage electrical repair, operating discreetly within the Zhujiang Road computer market (Two-Person IT Retailer & Repair Shop)." The owner (Zhang Chuanfei) most likely operates a small storefront or workshop within Weilai City, with the primary business consisting of selling mice and keyboards, providing computer and printer repair services, and occasionally undertaking minor office LAN cabling projects.
